easy.q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
What is the largest planet in the solar system?
Which planet has a famous ring system visible from Earth?
How many planets are in our solar system?
What is the closest planet to the Sun?
What is the name of Earth's natural satellite?
Which planet is the hottest in the solar system?
What star is at the center of our solar system?
What is the smallest planet in the solar system?
Which planet is farthest from the Sun?
What is the name of the largest volcano in the solar system, located on Mars?
Is Pluto considered a planet today?
easy.a: No, it is classified as a dwarf planet

easy.q: Which planet spins on its side, with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees?
What do we call a chunk of rock and ice that develops a glowing tail as it nears the Sun?
What is the name of the boundary in the solar system where the solar wind slows down as it meets interstellar space?
What is the name of the large storm on Jupiter that has raged for centuries?
Earth is the third planet from the Sun — true or false?
What is the main belt of rocky bodies between Mars and Jupiter called?
Which planet is known for its blue color caused by methane in its atmosphere?
easy.a: Uranus (also Neptune, but Uranus is the classic answer)

easy.q: How long does Earth take to orbit the Sun?
What is the name of the largest moon of Saturn?
Which planet has the most moons in the solar system?
What shape are the orbits of planets around the Sun?
Which planet rotates the fastest, completing a day in about 10 hours?
What is the thin layer of gases surrounding Earth called?
Does Mars have any moons?
What is the force that keeps planets in orbit around the Sun?
Which is larger: Earth or Mars?
What is the name given to the region beyond Neptune where Pluto lives?
Which planet is sometimes called Earth's twin because of its similar size?
What type of object is the Sun?
Which planet has a large canyon system called Valles Marineris?
What is the name of the closest star to our solar system?
How many Earth days does it take Mercury to orbit the Sun?
Which moon of Jupiter is the most volcanically active body in the solar system?
What do we call the path a planet follows around the Sun?
Is the Moon larger or smaller than Earth?
What do we call a small rocky body that burns up in Earth's atmosphere, creating a streak of light?
What is the name of the largest dwarf planet in the solar system?
easy.a: Eris (marginally larger in mass than Pluto)

easy.q: Which planet has the longest year, taking about 165 Earth years to orbit the Sun?
What is the common name for a meteor that reaches Earth's surface?
What is the name of the point in a planet's orbit farthest from the Sun?

Which direction do most planets orbit the Sun — clockwise or counterclockwise when viewed from above the North Pole?
What is the outer cloud of icy bodies at the edge of the solar system called?
What is the name of the rocky dwarf planet that is also the largest body in the asteroid belt?
What is the name of Jupiter's largest moon, the biggest moon in the solar system?
How many moons does Earth have?
Which planet has no moons and no rings?
What do we call the point in a planet's orbit when it is closest to the Sun?
What is the name of the largest impact crater on Mars, found in the southern hemisphere?
What is the name of the first dwarf planet discovered in the asteroid belt?
What is the name of the thin tail of gas and dust that points away from the Sun when a comet passes through the inner solar system?
What are the four planets closest to the Sun collectively called?
What is the name of the bright cloud of gas and dust surrounding the nucleus of a comet?

medium.q: What are Jupiter's four largest moons collectively called?
Which moon of Jupiter is the most volcanically active body in the solar system?
What is the name of the icy moon of Saturn that jets water vapor from its south pole?
Which planet has the lowest density — so low it would float in water?

Which dwarf planet was reclassified from full planet status in 2006?
What is the name of the largest canyon in the solar system, on Mars?
Which moon of Neptune orbits in the opposite direction to Neptune's rotation?
What is the name of Pluto's largest moon?
Which planet has a year lasting 84 Earth years?
What is the Great Red Spot on Jupiter?
Which gas giant is sometimes called an 'ice giant' along with Neptune?
How many moons does Mars have?
Which planet has the strongest winds in the solar system, reaching over 2,000 km/h?
What is the name of the region where most short-period comets originate?
Which of Jupiter's Galilean moons is the largest moon in the solar system?
What is the approximate distance from Earth to the Sun, known as 1 AU?
What is the name of the volcanic moon of Jupiter that has over 400 active volcanoes and mountains taller than Everest?
What is the name of the large dark spot observed on Neptune by Voyager 2 in 1989?
Which moon of Saturn has a thick nitrogen atmosphere, the only moon with a substantial atmosphere?
What is the Roche limit?
medium.a: The minimum distance at which a celestial body can approach a planet without being torn apart by tidal forces

medium.q: Which planet has the longest day in the solar system (in terms of rotation period), longer than its year?
What is the name of the largest asteroid in the asteroid belt?
medium.a: Ceres (also classified as a dwarf planet)

medium.q: Which of Saturn's moons is sometimes compared to early Earth due to its hydrocarbon lakes and atmosphere?
What is the name of the second-largest moon of Saturn, heavily cratered?
How does Venus rotate compared to most other planets?
What is the name of the boundary where the solar wind meets interstellar space?
Which two planets have no moons?
What is the name of the dark volcanic plains on the Moon?
Which moon of Jupiter is covered in a smooth icy shell and likely has a subsurface ocean?
What is the name of the feature on Mars where the Tharsis volcanoes are located?
What is the name of the shepherd moon of Neptune that keeps the Adams ring narrow?
What is the name of the region beyond the Kuiper Belt from which long-period comets originate?
Which dwarf planet beyond Neptune is slightly more massive than Pluto?
What is the name of the largest moon of Uranus?
How long does light from the Sun take to reach Earth?
What is the name of the largest volcano on Mars, and what type of volcano is it?
What is the name of the smaller of Mars's two moons, which orbits very close to Mars?
What is the name of Jupiter's moon that is thought to have a subsurface saltwater ocean?
medium.a: Callisto (also Europa, but Callisto is less often cited)

medium.q: Which planet's day is longer than its year — meaning it rotates more slowly than it orbits the Sun?
What is the name of the outermost large moon of Uranus, known for its dramatic cliffs?
What kind of object is Halley's Comet, and how often does it visit the inner solar system?
medium.a: A short-period comet; approximately every 75–76 years

medium.q: Which planet is third from the Sun?
What is the name of the layer of the Sun's atmosphere visible as a glowing halo during a solar eclipse?
Which Jovian moon is the only moon in the solar system known to be larger than the planet Mercury?
What is the name of the dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt discovered in 2005, which led to Pluto's reclassification?
Which planet emits more heat than it receives from the Sun?
What is the name of Saturn's shepherd moon that helps maintain the outer edge of the A ring?
What is the name of the impact basin on Mercury that is one of the largest in the solar system?
Which planet has a magnetic field roughly 20,000 times stronger than Earth's?
What is the name of the feature on the Moon that was formed by ancient lava flows?
How many dwarf planets are officially recognized by the IAU?
Which moon of Neptune is thought to be a captured Kuiper Belt object?
What is the name of the mechanism by which the Sun generates energy?
Which planet has a feature called the 'hexagonal storm' at its north pole?

hard.q: What is the approximate diameter of the Sun in kilometers?
Which moon of Jupiter was discovered by Galileo in 1610 along with Io, Europa, and Callisto?
What is the name of the gap in Saturn's rings between the A ring and B ring?

What is the eccentricity of a perfectly circular orbit?
Which spacecraft, launched in 1977, was the first human-made object to reach interstellar space?
What is the name of the smallest and innermost of the Galilean moons?
What is the approximate surface temperature of Venus in Celsius?
Which moon of Saturn is the second largest and has a density suggesting it is mostly water ice?
What is the name of the theoretical cloud of icy bodies extending up to 100,000 AU from the Sun?
Which dwarf planet beyond the Kuiper Belt has the most distant known orbit in the solar system (as of 2024)?
What is the name of the moon of Mars that is gradually spiraling inward and will eventually crash into Mars or break apart?
What is the Titius-Bode law?
hard.a: An empirical rule that predicts the spacing of planets from the Sun using a mathematical sequence

hard.q: Which Jovian moon has the highest known surface gravity of any moon?
What is the name of the process by which the Moon always keeps the same face toward Earth?
What is the approximate mass of Jupiter relative to all other planets combined?
hard.a: More than twice the mass of all other planets combined

hard.q: Which moon of Saturn has a two-toned coloring — one hemisphere dark, one bright — known since Cassini's 17th-century observations?
What is the name of the largest crater on the Moon, located on the far side?
What is the approximate length of the Valles Marineris canyon system on Mars?
hard.a: About 4,000 km (roughly the width of the United States)

hard.q: Which moon of Uranus is named after a character in Shakespeare's The Tempest and is known for Verona Rupes, possibly the tallest cliff in the solar system?
What is the name of the asteroid that struck Earth about 66 million years ago and contributed to the mass extinction?
What is the Lagrange point L2, and which notable telescope is stationed there?
hard.a: A gravitational equilibrium point 1.5 million km beyond Earth; the James Webb Space Telescope orbits there

hard.q: Which moon of Neptune has geysers erupting nitrogen gas from its surface?
What is the name of Saturn's F ring's inner shepherd moon, which orbits just inside the F ring?
What is the Kirkwood gaps?
hard.a: Gaps in the asteroid belt caused by orbital resonances with Jupiter

hard.q: Which dwarf planet has a reddish surface thought to be covered in tholins, and was discovered in 2005 by Mike Brown's team?
What is the approximate rotation period of the Sun at its equator in Earth days?
Which moon of Jupiter has a surface covered in salt flats and is heavily bombarded by Jupiter's radiation belts?
What is the name of the layer inside the Sun where energy is transported by convection rather than radiation?
What is the Hill sphere of a planet?
hard.a: The region around a planet where its gravity dominates over the Sun's gravity, allowing moons to orbit stably

hard.q: What is the name of the moon of Jupiter that has a subsurface ocean estimated to contain more liquid water than all of Earth's oceans combined?
What is the semi-major axis of Pluto's orbit in astronomical units (AU)?
Which asteroid was the first to be visited by a spacecraft (the NEAR Shoemaker mission in 1997)?
What is the name of the fifth-largest moon of Uranus, heavily fractured and possibly reassembled after a collision?
What is the approximate surface gravity on Mars compared to Earth?
Which moon of Saturn orbits within the Encke Gap of the A ring?
What is the name of the sunspot cycle and its approximate duration?
What is the Trojan group of asteroids, and which planet has the most?
hard.a: Asteroids sharing a planet's orbit at the L4 and L5 Lagrange points; Jupiter has the most

hard.q: Which moon of Neptune has a retrograde orbit suggesting it was captured from the Kuiper Belt?
What is the name of the high-speed stream of charged particles continuously emitted by the Sun?
What is the Poynting-Robertson effect?
hard.a: The process by which radiation pressure causes small dust particles to slowly spiral inward toward the Sun

hard.q: Which moon of Saturn is about 80% water ice and has a surface gravity so low that escape velocity is only about 400 m/s?
What is the name of the outermost named gap in Saturn's main rings, separating the A ring from the narrow F ring?
Which spacecraft performed the first flyby of Pluto in July 2015?
What is the approximate surface temperature of Pluto in Kelvin?
What is the name of the two largest near-Earth asteroid families, classified by orbit type crossing Earth's?
Which moon of Jupiter orbits farthest from Jupiter among the Galilean moons?
What is the name of the photosphere phenomenon where bright granules mark the tops of convection cells on the Sun?
Which feature of Mercury's surface is the result of the planet's core cooling and shrinking, causing the crust to buckle?
What is the Jeans escape mechanism in planetary atmospheres?
hard.a: The process by which lighter gas molecules moving faster than escape velocity leak away from the top of an atmosphere

hard.q: What is the name of the large oval impact structure on Mercury, one of the largest impact basins in the solar system?
What is the name of the effect by which a body's tidal forces cause the orbit of a nearby moon to gradually decay?
hard.a: Tidal dissipation (or orbital decay due to tidal friction)

hard.q: Which asteroid in the outer main belt is the target of the NASA Lucy mission launched in 2021?
What is the approximate number of moons Saturn has, as confirmed by astronomers by 2023?
What is the name of the phenomenon where Jupiter's gravity prevents a planet from forming in the asteroid belt?
